topic: Machining: 3 signs that your cutting tool needs sharpening.

manual_inputs.paragraph: Machining: 3 signs that your cutting tool needs sharpening.
manual_inputs.additional_details:
Explain the practical warning signs: abnormal overheating, vibrations/noise, and deteriorating surface quality such as burrs, streaks, or marks. Position Albert
  Descloux SA as a precise workshop partner that can diagnose the tool, check geometry, sharpen, and help stabilize production.

  Tone:
  Technical, clear, practical, workshop-focused.

  CTA:
  Ask users to send the material, tool type, symptom, and a photo of the cutting edge for a quick diagnosis.

  Useful link:
  https://www.desclouxsa.ch/contact/

  Image direction:
  A sharp, professional workshop scene showing a cutting tool or saw blade being inspected or sharpened, with clean lighting, visible precision equipment, and an
  industrial feel.
